summary: Suspicious string-splitting obfuscation in package using a brand-impersonating name
details: Package name impersonates Anthropic's Claude product and offers a 'free' version, a brand-confusion shape that warrants human review. The shipped lib/index.js contains a split-and-join construction of the literal 'base64' (['ba','se','64'].join(...)), a low-effort string-obfuscation pattern commonly used to hide base64 decoding of payloads or endpoints from casual review. No traced runtime evidence of credential theft, install-time fetch-and-execute, exfiltration to an attacker-controlled endpoint, or destructive install behavior was captured, so the concrete installer-harm mechanism is not established. Routing to human review so a reviewer can assess the brand-impersonation aspect and de-obfuscate the surrounding code path before issuing a public verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
